description: Public website + FastAPI backend + PyPI package for browsing and downloading weather data from Google Drive

stories.title: Project scaffold and structure
acceptanceCriteria: D:\Project\weather-website\frontend\index.html exists
D:\Project\weather-website\backend\main.py exists with FastAPI app
D:\Project\weather-website\package\pyproject.toml exists with name='TeamOverbyeWeather'
D:\Project\weather-website\backend\requirements.txt exists
D:\Project\weather-website\.env.example documents all required env vars

stories.title: Backend - Drive catalog endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/catalog returns JSON with keys: era5_na, era5_tx, hrrr_history, hrrr_forecast, noaa_forecast
era5_na and era5_tx have 'quarters' array (e.g. ['2024-Q1','2024-Q2'])
hrrr_history has 'months' array (e.g. ['2024-01','2024-02'])
hrrr_forecast and noaa_forecast have 'cycles' array (e.g. ['2026-04-21T06Z'])
Catalog is cached in memory and has a /api/catalog/refresh endpoint
backend/catalog.py contains DriveClient class with list_* methods

stories.title: Backend - Pipeline status endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/status returns JSON with keys: noaa, hrrr_forecast, hrrr_history, era5
Each value is one of: 'ok', 'warning', 'error', 'unknown'
Reads from ALERT_STATE_PATH env var (defaults to config/alert_state.json)
Returns 'unknown' for all pipelines when file is missing or unreadable
Maps alert_active:true -> 'error', alert_active:false -> 'ok'

stories.title: Backend - Download endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/download?source=era5_na&dates=2024-Q1 returns redirect to Drive webContentLink (single file)
GET /api/download?source=era5_na&dates=2024-Q1,2024-Q2 streams a ZIP bundle as application/zip
GET /api/download?source=hrrr_history&dates=2024-01 returns redirect for single month ZIP
GET /api/download?source=noaa_forecast&dates=2026-04-21T06Z returns redirect for single PWW file
backend/download.py contains build_zip_stream() that streams without storing full file on disk
Returns 404 with JSON error when requested files are not found in catalog

stories.title: Frontend - Pipeline status bar and Step 1 (Data Source)
acceptanceCriteria: Status bar at top of page shows colored dot + label for each pipeline (NOAA, HRRR Forecast, HRRR History, ERA5)
Green dot when status='ok', yellow when 'warning', red when 'error', grey when 'unknown'
Status auto-refreshes every 5 minutes via fetch('/api/status')
Step 1 shows 3 clickable cards: ERA5 (satellite icon), HRRR (radar icon), NOAA/GFS (globe icon)
Each card has a short description of the dataset
Clicking a card highlights it and reveals Step 2

stories.title: Frontend - Step 2 (Type) and Step 3 (Date picker)
acceptanceCriteria: Step 2 shows Type cards filtered by Step 1: ERA5 shows Historical only; HRRR shows Historical+Forecast; NOAA shows Forecast only
Step 3 renders quarter grid for ERA5 (Q1-Q4 x year), multi-select allowed
Step 3 renders month grid for HRRR Historical (clickable month cells), multi-select allowed
Step 3 renders scrollable cycle list for HRRR/NOAA Forecast (last 9 days x 4 cycles/day)
Selected dates highlighted with distinct style
Changing Step 1 or Step 2 resets Step 3 selection

stories.title: Frontend - Download bar and action
acceptanceCriteria: Download bar at bottom shows summary: e.g. 'ERA5 North America — Q1 2025, Q2 2025 (2 files → ZIP)'
Download button is disabled when no dates selected
Single file selected: clicking Download triggers redirect to /api/download which delivers file directly
Multiple files selected: clicking Download triggers ZIP stream download from /api/download
Loading spinner shown on Download button while request is in progress
Error message shown if /api/download returns non-200 response

stories.title: TeamOverbyeWeather Python package
acceptanceCriteria: package/TeamOverbyeWeather/__init__.py exports WeatherClient
WeatherClient(base_url='https://...') accepts optional base_url
client.era5.list_quarters(region='north_america') returns list of quarter strings
client.era5.download(region='north_america', quarters=['2025-Q1'], dest='./data/') downloads file
client.noaa.download_latest(dest='./data/') downloads most recent NOAA forecast
client.status() returns dict with pipeline health
package/pyproject.toml has correct metadata, dependencies (requests, tqdm)
package/README.md has installation and usage examples

stories.title: Deployment configuration and documentation
acceptanceCriteria: backend/Dockerfile builds a working FastAPI container (FROM python:3.12-slim)
backend/docker-compose.yml mounts alert_state.json from host path via ALERT_STATE_PATH
.env.example documents: GDRIVE_CREDENTIALS_JSON, GDRIVE_ROOT_FOLDER_ID, ALERT_STATE_PATH, CORS_ORIGINS
README.md at project root documents: architecture diagram, local dev setup, Cloudflare Pages deployment steps, Railway/Render backend deploy steps
frontend/README.md documents how to point the frontend at a custom API URL
